title: Certificate is worthless
body: Do not choose USCI, I had a bad experience. My certificate is worthless bc employers dont like their low standards of teaching. Its cheap bc its worthless for many. No credits transfer to my local college i had to start over. Plus I just saw they had shut down and changed names so if that happens again you're loss.

title: Lowest standards
body: I did not have a good experience with this school and legitimately its the lowest standards so if you are fine being low standard and poorly trained go for it but I suggest a school with higher accreditation standards.

title: Did not prepare for CPC exam
body: I got my training from them. They do not adequately train for the cpc exam. I had to teach myself and buy the AAPC study guide and practice exams. The only good thing about USCI program is that my job gave tuition reimbursement, and completing the program counted as a year off my apprenticeship.

title: Certification is useless
body: I wouldn't recommend it as it's not AHIMA accredited and it gives you a credential that is useless. Healthcare systems don't recognize the CBCS certification. They want CCS, CCS-P or CPC etc. from AAPC or AHIMA.

title: Certificate only, not a real credential
body: The US Career Institute Course will only give you a certificate in medical billing and coding. Please know this certificate is not an actual credential. You will then need to go on and study for a credentialing exam through either AHIMA or AAPC. You may be able to get a basic coding education through the US Career Institute however I do not believe that they prepare you for any type of credentialing exam.

title: Program useless without clinical hours - refused refund
body: Purchased an online MA program on 5.13.25. Did program and testing, never received certification. However I find out that this program is useless to me as I do not have clinical hours. I would like a partial or full refund as I will need in person clinical hours.

title: No externships — employers denied me
body: I saw somewhere people had been denied a job simply for attending that program, its not accredited, have poor customer service... and I don't want to take my chances with it.

title: No externship offered
body: I forgot to mention if you have the option to do Stepful, it might be better since they offer externships. I did the USCI program in like 4 days then bought the smarterMA study course and I feel like I've learned more from it than USCI.

title: No externships — consider this carefully
body: Also, AFAIK USCI doesn't do externships so that might be something to consider!

title: Want externships which USCI doesn't offer
body: I want to be able to get my hands on externships, which I have heard USCI does not offer, but Stepful is very fast-paced and strict on scheduling (At the moment I can not work that) but has amazing reviews from what I've seen.
